title: Global Obesity Trends: A Complex Picture of Health
subtitle: New research shows that while obesity rates are slowing in wealthy nations, the global health challenge remains urgent.

summary.en: A massive new study shows that obesity rates are changing in different ways across the world. While many wealthy nations are seeing a slowdown in weight gain, obesity continues to rise in lower-income countries. Researchers warn that this global health issue is not the same everywhere. Experts emphasize that the speed of weight gain is now a more important measure than just looking at total numbers. Additionally, new findings link adult weight gain to a significantly higher risk of developing various types of cancer.
summary.zh: 一項大規模的新研究顯示，全球各地的肥胖率正以不同方式改變。雖然許多富裕國家的體重增加速度正在放緩，但低收入國家的肥胖率卻持續上升。研究人員警告，這項全球健康議題在各地的情況並不相同。專家強調，體重增加的速度現在比單純觀察總人數更具指標意義。此外，新發現將成年後的體重增加與罹患多種癌症的風險顯著提高連結了起來。

paragraphs.en: For decades, the world has faced a growing health crisis known as the obesity epidemic. However, a major new study suggests that the situation is far more complex than a simple, one-size-fits-all label. According to research published in the journal Nature, which analyzed data from 232 million people across 45 years, the rise of obesity is not inevitable. In many high-income countries, the rapid increase in obesity rates is beginning to slow down, plateau, or even potentially decline.
paragraphs.zh: 數十年來，全球面臨著一場日益嚴重的健康危機，即肥胖流行病。然而，一項重大的新研究指出，情況遠比單一且一體適用的標籤來得複雜。根據發表在《自然》期刊上的研究，該研究分析了四十五年來二點三二億人的數據，肥胖的增加並非不可避免。在許多高收入國家，肥胖率的快速成長已開始放緩、進入高原期，甚至可能出現下降。

paragraphs.en: Majid Ezzati, a professor at Imperial College London and the leader of the study, noted that the diversity of these trends is surprising. Even countries with similar economic or technological backgrounds show very different results. For example, while obesity rates have reached a plateau in countries like Germany, they may have started to fall in France. In contrast, the United States continues to see rising obesity rates among adults, even though the trend has stabilized among children and teenagers. According to STAT News, the U.S. currently ranks among the highest in the world for obesity, with rates between 40% and 43% for adults.
paragraphs.zh: 倫敦帝國學院 (Imperial College London) 教授、同時也是該研究負責人的馬吉德·埃扎蒂 (Majid Ezzati) 指出，這些趨勢的多樣性令人驚訝。即使是經濟或技術背景相似的國家，結果也大不相同。例如，德國等國的肥胖率已進入高原期，而法國的肥胖率可能已開始下降。相比之下，美國成年人的肥胖率持續上升，儘管兒童與青少年的趨勢已趨於穩定。根據 STAT 新聞報導，美國目前的肥胖率位居全球最高之列，成年人肥胖率介於百分之四十至百分之四十三之間。

paragraphs.en: While wealthy nations are seeing some progress, the situation in many low- and middle-income countries is moving in the opposite direction. Researchers report that in these parts of the world, obesity rates are still climbing steadily, and in some cases, the speed of this increase is accelerating. This is a major concern for global health experts, as rising obesity is closely linked to serious health problems like diabetes and heart disease. Naveed Sattar, a professor at the University of Glasgow, warned that the rapid rise in these developing nations is especially worrying because it could lead to a significant increase in chronic illnesses.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管富裕國家已取得一些進展，但許多低收入與中等收入國家的情況卻朝相反方向發展。研究人員報告稱，在世界這些地區，肥胖率仍在穩定攀升，且在某些情況下，增加的速度正在加快。這對全球健康專家來說是一大隱憂，因為肥胖增加與糖尿病和心臟病等嚴重健康問題密切相關。格拉斯哥大學 (University of Glasgow) 教授納維德·薩塔爾 (Naveed Sattar) 警告說，這些開發中國家的快速成長尤其令人擔憂，因為這可能導致慢性疾病顯著增加。

paragraphs.en: Experts are now focusing on the "velocity" of obesity, which measures how fast weight gain is happening rather than just looking at the total number of people affected. By using this approach, researchers hope to better understand why some countries are succeeding in slowing the trend while others are not. The reasons are likely a mix of social, economic, and policy factors. These could include everything from how easy it is to buy unhealthy, processed food to the presence of school programs that provide healthy meals to children.
paragraphs.zh: 專家們現在正聚焦於肥胖的「速度」，這項指標衡量的是體重增加的快慢，而不僅僅是觀察受影響的總人數。透過這種方法，研究人員希望更深入了解為何有些國家能成功減緩趨勢，而其他國家卻無法做到。原因很可能是社會、經濟與政策因素的綜合影響。這些因素可能涵蓋各個層面，從購買不健康加工食品的便利性，到學校是否提供兒童健康餐點的計畫等。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the immediate health concerns of weight gain, new research has highlighted a dangerous long-term consequence: cancer. A study presented at the European Congress on Obesity in Istanbul found that gaining weight as an adult can increase the risk of certain cancers by up to five times. Researchers at Lund University in Sweden tracked more than 600,000 people and found that there is no "safe age" to gain excess weight. The study showed that the higher the starting weight and the more weight gained during adulthood, the higher the risk of developing various cancers, including liver, pancreatic, and colon cancer.
paragraphs.zh: 除了體重增加帶來的直接健康疑慮外，新研究還強調了一個危險的長期後果：癌症。在伊斯坦堡舉行的歐洲肥胖大會 (European Congress on Obesity) 上發表的一項研究發現，成年後體重增加會使罹患特定癌症的風險提高多達五倍。瑞典隆德大學 (Lund University) 的研究人員追蹤了超過六十萬人，發現體重過重並沒有所謂的「安全年齡」。研究顯示，成年後的起始體重越高、增加的體重越多，罹患各種癌症（包括肝癌、胰臟癌與大腸癌）的風險就越高。

paragraphs.en: For men, the link between weight gain and cancer was strongest for those who put on weight before the age of 45. For women, gaining weight after the age of 30 was strongly associated with an increased risk of cancers related to hormones, such as breast and endometrial cancer. Anton Nilsson, the lead author of the study, explained that early weight gain might give the body more time to develop harmful biological processes, such as inflammation and higher insulin levels, which can damage tissues over time.
paragraphs.zh: 對男性而言，體重增加與癌症之間的關聯，在四十五歲前增重者身上最為強烈。對女性而言，三十歲後的體重增加與荷爾蒙相關癌症（如乳癌與子宮內膜癌）的風險增加有強烈關聯。該研究的主要作者安東·尼爾森 (Anton Nilsson) 解釋說，早期體重增加可能會讓身體有更多時間發展出有害的生物過程，例如發炎與胰島素水平升高，這些過程會隨時間推移損害組織。

paragraphs.en: Despite these findings, some experts remain cautious about how to interpret the data. For instance, William Dietz from George Washington of George Washington University of George Washington University told STAT News that he found the approach of looking at year-to-year changes in body mass index (BMI) to be an unusual way to track the epidemic. Others, like David Allison from Baylor College of Medicine, noted that confirming obesity is rising in most places is not new information. However, the study authors argue that their detailed analysis provides a necessary map for policymakers to understand where interventions are working and where they are failing.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管有這些發現，一些專家對於如何解讀數據仍持保留態度。例如，喬治華盛頓大學 (George Washington University) 的威廉·迪茨 (William Dietz) 向 STAT 新聞表示，他認為觀察身體質量指數 (BMI) 的年度變化來追蹤流行病的方式並不尋常。其他人，如貝勒醫學院 (Baylor College of Medicine) 的大衛·艾利森 (David Allison) 則指出，確認肥胖在多數地方呈上升趨勢並非新資訊。然而，研究作者主張，他們詳盡的分析為決策者提供了一份必要的藍圖，以了解哪些干預措施有效，哪些則失敗了。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the challenge for global health officials is to identify which specific strategies are helping wealthy nations stabilize their obesity rates. As the world continues to change, understanding these local differences will be essential. Whether through better food policies, increased physical activity, or public health education, the goal remains the same: to stop the rise of obesity and protect people from the long-term health risks associated with it.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，全球衛生官員面臨的挑戰是找出哪些具體策略有助於富裕國家穩定其肥胖率。隨著世界持續變遷，理解這些地區差異將至關重要。無論是透過更好的食品政策、增加身體活動，還是公共衛生教育，目標始終如一：遏止肥胖的蔓延，並保護民眾免受其帶來的長期健康風險。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To reach a state of little or no change after a period of activity or progress.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 達到穩定狀態，不再增長或下降。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（高原），這裡作動詞用。文中：In many high-income countries, the rapid increase in obesity rates is beginning to slow down, plateau, or even potentially decline.
常見作「穿上」衣服，這裡指體重增加。文中：For men, the link between weight gain and cancer was strongest for those who put on weight before the age of 45.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Actions taken to improve a medical or social situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 干預措施（指為了改善現狀而採取的行動）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 在公共衛生語境下，指針對特定問題採取的政策或行動。文中：However, the study authors argue that their detailed analysis provides a necessary map for policymakers to understand where interventions are working and where they are failing.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Obesity rates in some countries levelling off or potentially falling, study finds

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: The ‘velocity of obesity’ aims to show which nations are slowing an epidemic

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Weight gain as adult increases cancer risk by up to five times, research shows

quiz.question: According to the study presented at the European Congress on Obesity, how does the timing of weight gain affect cancer risk?

options.A: It only impacts cancer risk if weight is gained after the age of 45.
options.B: Men face a higher cancer risk if they gain weight before age 45, while women face higher risks if they gain weight after age 30.
options.C: Weight gain after age 30 is the primary cause of liver and pancreatic cancer for all adults.
options.D: There is no significant difference in cancer risk based on the age at which weight gain occurs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第六段，研究指出男性在 45 歲前增重與癌症關聯最強，而女性則是在 30 歲後增重與荷爾蒙相關癌症（如乳癌）關聯密切。選項 A 錯誤，因為 45 歲前增重對男性影響更大；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章未指明 30 歲後增重是所有癌症的唯一主因；選項 D 與文章中強調年齡與生物過程（如發炎）的關聯相矛盾。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the effectiveness of current obesity interventions globally?

options.A: All countries have successfully implemented policies that have stopped the rise of obesity.
options.B: Low-income countries are currently using the exact same strategies as high-income countries.
options.C: The success of obesity reduction strategies is highly dependent on local social and economic contexts.
options.D: Wealthy nations have completely eliminated the health risks associated with obesity.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第二段提到即使經濟背景相似的國家，肥胖趨勢也大不相同；第四段進一步指出肥胖趨勢的差異是社會、經濟與政策因素混合的結果。這暗示沒有單一的全球解決方案，必須考量在地因素。選項 A 錯誤，因為美國等國肥胖率仍在上升；選項 B 文章未提及；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章僅提到部分國家趨勢趨緩，而非消除了健康風險。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'velocity' mean as it is used to describe obesity?

options.A: The physical movement or activity level of obese individuals.
options.B: The speed or rate at which weight gain occurs over time.
options.C: The total percentage of the population currently classified as obese.
options.D: The intensity of the medical treatment required for weight loss.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到專家現在關注肥胖的「velocity」，並解釋這是在測量體重增加的速度（how fast weight gain is happening），而非單純看總人數。選項 A 是對 velocity 的字面誤解；選項 C 是指 prevalence（盛行率）；選項 D 與文中對該詞的定義無關。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main message of the article?

options.A: The global obesity epidemic is a uniform problem that requires a single, standardized international policy.
options.B: Weight gain is only a concern for people living in high-income, developed nations.
options.C: Obesity trends are complex and varied, requiring a nuanced understanding of local factors and long-term health consequences.
options.D: Scientific research has proven that it is impossible to stop the rise of obesity in any country.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章開頭即強調肥胖問題比想像中複雜，並透過分析各國趨勢差異（高收入 vs. 低收入國家）、強調社會政策因素以及長期癌症風險，說明了理解肥胖問題需要多面向的視角。選項 A 與文章強調的「差異性」相左；選項 B 錯誤，因為文章提到低收入國家肥胖率正在加速上升；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章提到某些高收入國家已成功讓肥胖率趨緩或下降。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
